notes: Exceptional execution across all criteria. Agent extracted all significant dates (Effective Date, 7 project phases, 6 payment milestones, 12-month warranty, 5-year confidentiality survival, 12-month non-solicitation, 48-hour breach notification, 30-day cure period, 60-day termination notice, 90-day force majeure) in clear chronological order. Party obligations section thoroughly cataloged 10+ Provider obligations (ERP delivery, CCPA/GDPR/SOC 2 compliance, 48-hour breach notification, annual audits, 12-month warranty, indemnification) and 10+ Client obligations (milestone payments, system access, cooperation) with clear organization by party. Risk analysis identified 12 Client risks, 10 Provider risks, and 5 shared risks with sophisticated legal reasoning—e.g., liability cap limits Client recourse for consequential damages; IP transfer conditional on full payment creates ownership gaps; binding arbitration in Austin disadvantages California Client; no change-order process; uncapped IP indemnity for Provider. Financial summary accurately captured $2.4M total with all six installments, correct due dates, payment percentages, front-loading analysis (30% before development), 1.5% monthly late fees, and 10% retainage provision. File properly saved to contract_analysis.md with professional formatting. Minor inefficiency: two nearly identical analysis files created (contract_analysis.md and contract_analysis_1.md), suggesting unnecessary revision, but both outputs are substantively complete and accurate. Analysis demonstrates genuine legal reasoning beyond mere contract summarization.
